V
Veil
tunnel · isolate · forge
Run
Configure
Inspect
System
free

Profiles

Network-isolated app profiles. Each runs in its own namespace with its own chain.

No profiles yet

Create one by hand or bulk-import a folder of WireGuard / OpenVPN configs.

New profile

Define how an app reaches the network and what identity it presents.
Use case

Pick the closest fit. Each preset auto-fills sensible defaults and hides fields you don't need. You can tweak everything from Custom.

Identity
Network chain

Hops apply in order from your host to the exit. Each hop runs inside the profile's namespace.

Application
Network policy
Anti-detect Pro

Two modes that compose: cohort blending (look like every other RFP user) and/or impersonation (look like a specific real device). Use either or both — when both are set, the persona's specific values feed the JS extension and the strict-tier TLS/HTTP mediation + per-profile CA still apply.

Combined mode active. Persona's specific values (UA / GPU / screen / timezone) win over the generic blend in the JS extension. Strict-tier TLS handshake spoofing, HTTP mediator, and per-profile CA still apply. This is the strongest configuration.

basic: cohort-blend at JS layer + TCP fingerprint persona + time namespace + kill switch. No CA install.
strict: everything in basic, plus a per-profile TLS interception CA so the wire-level TLS handshake matches your persona. The CA is installed only into this profile's data dir (your normal browsers stay untouched). One-time consent dialog on first launch.

Forge constraints (all optional — empty = auto-pick) 


          
          
          
          
        


        

          Opsec — endpoint pinning Pro
          
Lock the network exit per profile. The profile refuses to launch if the actual exit drifts from what's expected.

          
          
Tick what you want to lock. Each is independent — combine any subset. The corresponding input shows only when its box is ticked; leave blank to auto-fill (country from persona at launch, ASN/IP captured on first successful verification).

          
          

            
          

          
          

            
          

          
          

            
          

          
          
        


        

          DNS shape Pro
          
By default Veil routes DNS via the chain (Tor's DNSPort or the proxy). With dns_match_exit on, every lookup is forced through DoH to an anycast resolver, riding the chain — so the resolver IP visible to upstream auth-NS records lands geographically at the chain's exit. Whoer-style "DNS doesn't match exit" tests then pass without weakening leak protection. The transparent DNS REDIRECT stays in place as a safety net.

          
          
          
          
          
When on, spawns veil dohproxy inside the profile's netns. ALL DNS queries (browser primary, OCSP, captive-portal probes, anything) get routed through it as DoH to the upstream below — no exceptions, no fallback to Tor's DNSPort. Closes the side-query leak surface (~5% of DNS in a typical session). Built into Veil; no extra package install required.

          
          
Requires at least one tunnel hop (WireGuard / OpenVPN / Tor). Without one, DoH packets travel direct from your real network — that would leak your host IP to the resolver and defeat the purpose. Validation refuses unsafe configs.

        


        

          
          
        

        

      

    


    
    

      

        

          
Bulk import

          
Drop a folder of WireGuard .conf or OpenVPN .ovpn files. Veil creates one profile per server.

        

      

      

        
        
        
        
        

          
        

        

      

    


    
    

      

        

          
Doctor

          
System checks: kernel features, capabilities, GeoIP DB, audit dir, depot tools.

    About
    
          
    Per-app tunnel isolation + anti-detect identity engine.
    
        
    
      
    

      
    
        
    
          
    
            
    License
    
            
    free
    
            
    
          
    
          Upgrade to Pro
        
    
      
    

      
    Local-first. No telemetry. Profiles stored as YAML under your user config dir; audit log + crash reports under ~/.config/veil/.
    

      
    
        Version dev
        source
        issues
      
    

      
    
        Install license
        
    Paste the contents of your license.jwt file. Validation is offline.
